summary: Tested the new token-based RE share-link feature (replaces Prospect Login). Backend: 15/15 pytest cases pass (100%) covering generate, get-status, public-view, book-appointment, regenerate-re and timeline. Frontend: public /quote/:token route renders correctly — live quote with expiry chip, project details, inclusions, Call Sales CTA; invalid tokens show 'Link unavailable' page; no auth cookies needed. CRMSales detail-dialog UI interaction could not be exercised live (specific lead 'Priya M' was not visible in the default CRMSales board view during the demo-admin session), but all required data-testids exist in the source code and the APIs those buttons invoke are validated backend-side.

issues: When re_project.estimated_total is 0/missing, the 'Approved Estimate' hero + 'Inclusions' total block collapse silently — consider showing 'Estimate pending' to avoid an empty-looking quote.

action_items: Optional UX: show a fallback 'Estimate pending finalisation' chip on the public quote view when estimated_total is 0 so the hero card isn't visually empty.
Optional: increase default lead visibility on /crm-sales board (or add a filter) so GM-approved/RE-sent leads like 'Priya M' (stg_re_request) show in the default view for quicker testing/demo.
Main agent can self-verify the remaining CRMSales detail-dialog flow by opening a lead with active_quote_token set and clicking detail-generate-re-link-btn / detail-regenerate-re-btn / tab-timeline.
critical_code_review_comments: quote_links.py: generate-quote-link correctly auto-revokes prior live links via update_many before inserting new doc — prior token returns 410 as expected.
public_get_quote: HMAC signature check prevents forged tokens (invalid & malformed → 404). Public endpoint works without cookies (withCredentials:false in PublicQuoteView publicAxios).

regenerate-re: correctly increments revision, points lead to new RE, moves stage to stg_re_request, creates Planning notification. New RE inherits rough_requirement/scope_items and stores previous_re_project_id for audit.
book-appointment: creates fresh lead tagged ['client_appointment','returning_prospect'], assigns to original sales_user_id, stores previous_lead_id — clean trail. Notification fires.
timeline endpoint sorts events newest-first and includes stage_history + follow_ups + appointment + advance_payment + quote_links — assertion verified.
quote_links.py uses a module-level AsyncIOMotorClient instead of the shared app client — harmless but inconsistent with the rest of routes/. Consider DRY-ing via core.deps.get_db.
Legacy prospect.py / CreateProspectUserDialog kept intentionally but now unused — cleanup candidate for a later pass.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (public view fully verified; CRMSales detail-dialog test-ids confirmed in code, not exercised live)

test_credentials: Super Admin via Demo Access tab → 'Login as Demo User' (admin@constructionos.com). Password login with Demo@1234 returned 401 on /api/auth/login for the demo admin; backend test fixture logs in successfully via the same endpoint, so the 401 seems UI-only; demo tab path works reliably.
seed_data_creation: Test created 1 new lead 'TEST_Returning Prospect' via public book-appointment endpoint (tagged client_appointment, linked to lead_73896054d131). No cleanup performed — low volume.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend route prefix for quote_links is /api/ (not /api/quote-links/). GM-approved fixture REs are re_2b9ca650e828 (lead_73896054d131) and re_036b7016e31c (lead_d08bc1f28f16); the fixture in test_quote_links.py resets lead.re_project_id to these approved IDs before each module run because prior regen runs re-point the lead to new in-progress REs. Public endpoints require no auth. The token format is 'quote_id.hmac_sig[:24]'.
